name: Corral de los Lobos y Tejerina desde Prioro – Parque regional Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre
region_intro: Hiking around Prioro offers access to diverse landscapes within the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park, characterized by majestic mountain ranges, expansive reservoirs, and verdant valleys. The terrain includes alpine limestone mountains, dense ancient beech forests, and deep river gorges. Towering peaks like Pico Gilbo and Pico Yordas provide challenging ascents with extensive mountain vistas. This region presents a blend of gray, green, and ocher hues, vertical summits, and intricate rock formations. Best hiking trails around Prioro The most popular hiking route is Senda de la Mitología Leonesa - circular from Carande through the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This easy route explores varied landscapes within the regional park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valle de Salio – circular from Carande through the Regional Park Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail offers views of the valley and surrounding natural features. Local hikers also love the Pico Gilbo from Horcadas — circular, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Prioro is defined by majestic mountain ranges, expansive reservoirs, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Prioro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 360 reviews. More than 2,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Prioro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Prioro?
A: Prioro offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 60 trails available. These routes cater to all skill levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Prioro?
A: The terrain around Prioro is incredibly diverse, ranging from majestic alpine limestone mountains to lush Atlantic meadows and dense ancient beech forests. You'll encounter deep gorges carved by rivers, expansive reservoirs, and dramatic rock formations, all within the stunning Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for families in Prioro?
A: Yes, Prioro has several easy hiking options perfect for families. One popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050055715/senda-de-la-mitologia-leonesa-circular-from-carande-through-the-regional-park-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Mitología Leonesa&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.5-mile (5.7 km) circular trail that explores varied landscapes within the regional park and is generally completed in under two hours.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes with significant elevation in Prioro?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region offers demanding ascents to peaks like Pico Gilbo and Pico Yordas. A notable challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383571186/pico-gilbo-from-horcadas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Gilbo from Horcadas — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.2-mile (6.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, typically taking around 3 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Prioro?
A: Yes, many trails in Prioro are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Beyond the family-friendly Senda de la Mitología Leonesa, you can explore the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050063229/valle-de-salio-circular-from-carande-through-the-regional-park-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle de Salio – circular from Carande&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful views of the valley and natural features.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Prioro?
A: The best times for hiking in Prioro are spring and autumn, when temperatures are pleasant and the landscapes are vibrant with colors. Summers are mild and ideal for exploring, while winters bring occasional snow, offering opportunities for snow activities in addition to hiking.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Prioro?
A: Prioro is rich in natural beauty and landmarks. You can encounter the stunning Riaño reservoir, often called the 'Leonese fjords,' and enjoy panoramic views from spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443514" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcayo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Towering peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/306845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gilbo Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yordas&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive mountain vistas. The region also features ancient beech forests and deep river gorges.

Q: Is Prioro a good place for wildlife observation during hikes?
A: Yes, Prioro and the surrounding Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park boast incredible biodiversity. It's a Zone of Special Protection for Birds (ZEPA) and a Site of Community Importance (LIC), making it an excellent area for observing native species. The park is also part of a recovery plan for the Cantabrian brown bear and capercaillie.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Prioro?
A: The trails in Prioro are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 360 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the well-marked paths, and the stunning mountain and reservoir views that make each outing a memorable experience.

Q: Are there dog-friendly trails in Prioro?
A: While the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park generally welcomes dogs, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Specific rules may vary, so check local signage or park regulations before your visit.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, you can combine your hikes with cultural exploration. The area features sites like the hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Pando, a beautiful example of religious architecture, and the remains of old water mills, which reflect the region's rich history and traditions.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on trails around Prioro?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly across Prioro's trails. Easy routes like the Senda de la Mitología Leonesa have modest gains of around 217 meters, while moderate trails such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050046120/puerto-de-horcadas-circular-by-the-regional-park-montana-de-riano-y-mampodre-prc-le-25" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Horcadas — circular&lt;/a&gt; can involve around 430 meters. Challenging ascents to peaks like Pico Gilbo can exceed 450 meters of elevation gain.
tips:
Located at an altitude of 1300m, the Corral de los Lobos is a facility for hunting wolves, which endanger the lives of cattle. From this point, we have splendid views of the Prioro and Tejerina valleys.
The Corral de los Lobos belongs to the most popular circular route in Prioro, which is about 3km away.

Located within the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park and belonging to the municipality of Prioro, Tejerina is a beautiful Leonese town where we can stop to rest or have a drink while visiting ''Las Lavanderas'' or ''Fuente del Pastor''.
Beautiful forest track accompanied by large oaks between Prioro and Tejerina. The shade of the trees will be ideal for the hottest days.
